channel.title: Scientific Computing & Software Engineering on YIN CHEANG Pages
channel.description: Recent content in Scientific Computing & Software Engineering on YIN CHEANG Pages

item.title: Lattice Boltzmann Method: From Theory to Practice
item.description:
Introduction

The Lattice Boltzmann Method (LBM) has emerged as a powerful alternative to traditional computational fluid dynamics (CFD) approaches. Unlike conventional methods that directly solve the Navier-Stokes equations, LBM is based on kinetic theory and statistical physics, offering a unique perspective on fluid dynamics simulation.

Theoretical Foundation

The Boltzmann Equation

At the heart of LBM lies the Boltzmann equation, which describes the evolution of a particle distribution function f(x, ξ, t):

item.title: Efficient Solutions for Numerical Linear Systems
item.description:
Efficient Solutions for Numerical Linear Systems

In scientific computing and engineering applications, solving large-scale linear systems is a fundamental challenge. This post explores various efficient methods and best practices for tackling these problems.

Direct Methods

LU Decomposition

LU decomposition is a powerful direct method for solving linear systems Ax = b. It factors matrix A into lower (L) and upper (U) triangular matrices:

  • Advantages:
    • Exact solution (barring numerical errors)
    • Efficient for multiple right-hand sides
    • Well-suited for dense matrices

Cholesky Decomposition

For symmetric positive-definite matrices, Cholesky decomposition offers a more efficient alternative:

item.title: Fast Multipole Method: Accelerating N-Body Simulations
item.description:
Introduction to Fast Multipole Method

The Fast Multipole Method (FMM) is a revolutionary algorithm that reduces the computational complexity of N-body problems from O(N²) to O(N). This breakthrough enables efficient simulation of large-scale particle systems and field calculations.

Key Concepts

  1. Multipole Expansion

    • Hierarchical representation of particle interactions
    • Far-field approximations
    • Error-controlled truncation

  2. Tree-based Structure

    • Octree decomposition
    • Near-field direct interactions
    • Far-field approximations

Integration with Direct Solvers

FASTSolver combines FMM with advanced direct solvers to provide a comprehensive solution for large-scale scientific computing:

item.title: FASTSolver Development Roadmap: Vision and Future Milestones
item.description:
Vision

FASTSolver aims to revolutionize scientific computing by providing a seamless, high-performance framework that bridges the gap between ease of use and computational efficiency. Our roadmap outlines the strategic development path toward achieving this vision.

Current State (2025 Q1)

Core Features

  • Hybrid Python/C++ architecture

    • Python frontend with NumPy-compatible API:
      • Full compatibility with NumPy’s ndarray operations
      • Support for broadcasting and advanced indexing
      • Array operations with minimal overhead (<5% compared to native NumPy)
      • Transparent data type conversion and memory management
      • Just-in-time compilation for critical code paths
    • C++ computational backend:
      • Optimized computational core using modern C++20 features
      • SIMD vectorization with AVX-512 instruction set
      • Cache-friendly data structures with aligned memory access
      • Template metaprogramming for compile-time optimizations
      • Performance >80% of pure C++ implementations
    • Automated type conversion and memory management:
      • Zero-copy data transfer between Python and C++ layers using Pybind11
      • Intelligent memory pooling for frequent allocations
      • Custom allocator with thread-local caching
      • Reference counting with cycle detection
      • Automatic memory defragmentation

  • Advanced numerical solvers

item.title: Fast Direct Solvers: Advanced Techniques for Linear Systems
item.description:
Introduction to Fast Direct Solvers

Fast direct solvers represent a crucial advancement in computational linear algebra, offering exact solutions to linear systems while maintaining computational efficiency. Unlike traditional direct methods, these advanced techniques leverage sophisticated mathematical and algorithmic approaches to achieve near-optimal performance.

Key Techniques

Sparse LU Factorization

Modern sparse LU factorization methods form the backbone of fast direct solvers:

  • Fill-in Minimization

    • Advanced ordering techniques (Nested Dissection, AMD)
    • Symbolic factorization optimization
    • Memory-efficient storage schemes

  • Supernodal Techniques

item.title: FASTSolver - Advanced Scientific Computing Framework
item.description:
About FASTSolver

FASTSolver is a revolutionary framework designed to make scientific computing accessible without compromising on performance. Born from the challenges faced by researchers and engineers in the field, FASTSolver combines ease of use with powerful computational capabilities.

Core Features

1. Hybrid Architecture

  • Python interface for intuitive programming
  • C++ core engine for high-performance computing
  • Seamless integration between ease of use and speed

2. Comprehensive Toolset

  • Advanced numerical solvers
  • Built-in visualization capabilities
  • Extensive scientific computing libraries
  • Real-time data processing

3. Domain-Specific Solutions

  • Fluid dynamics simulations
  • Complex mathematical computations
  • Data analysis and visualization
  • High-performance computing integration

Applications

Fluid Dynamics

  • Aircraft design optimization
  • Weather pattern prediction
  • Ocean current modeling
  • Aerodynamics simulation

Scientific Computing

  • Large-scale equation systems
  • Iterative problem-solving
  • Multi-dimensional calculations
  • Parallel computing support

Technical Details

Performance Optimization

  • Multi-threading capabilities
  • GPU acceleration support
  • Memory-efficient algorithms
  • Scalable architecture

Integration Capabilities

  • RESTful API support
  • Cloud deployment options
  • Container compatibility
  • Extensive documentation

Getting Started

Visit our GitHub repository for installation instructions, documentation, and examples.

item.title: About
item.description:
Professional Overview

I am a dedicated Software Engineer and researcher with expertise in scientific computing and numerical methods, backed by education from prestigious institutions including Tsinghua University and Beihang University. My work focuses on developing high-performance computing solutions, particularly in computational fluid dynamics and aerodynamic design optimization. With a strong foundation in both theoretical and applied aspects of scientific computing, I specialize in creating efficient algorithms and numerical simulations for complex engineering problems.
